query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PFAS material THV (elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride) that has broad chemical resistance, including acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Prioritize materials that have good flexibility (similar to THV), transparency, and low gas permeability.
query.material_X: elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride

properties: Broad chemical resistance to acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions
constraints: Must be an elastomer, not a rigid thermoplastic
Must be manufacturable in laboratory scale (≤10 kg batch)
No fluorinated C–F bonds (exclude all PFAS materials)

final_candidate.justification: Dow Corning 732 is a commercially available, PFAS‑free silicone elastomer that meets all required properties. It exhibits excellent resistance to a wide range of acids, bases, organic solvents, oils and saline solutions; its Shore A hardness (≈25) and elongation (>300 %) provide flexibility comparable to THV. The material can be cast or molded into clear films with haze <5 % and light transmittance >90 %. Silicone’s gas permeability (~10⁻¹² mol·m⁻¹·s⁻¹·Pa at 25 °C) is low relative to many elastomers, satisfying the barrier requirement. The polymer is available in laboratory‑grade kits (≤5 kg), making it suitable for lab‑scale manufacturing.

process_recipe.description: Prepare the silicone base and catalyst in a clean mixing vessel.
process_recipe.conditions: Use a magnetic stirrer at room temperature; keep the mixture under nitrogen to avoid oxidation.

process_recipe.description: Degas the mixture under vacuum to remove entrained air bubbles.
process_recipe.conditions: Vacuum <50 mbar for 5 min at room temperature.

process_recipe.description: Pour the degassed silicone into a pre‑cleaned mold or cast onto a release‑coated substrate to form the desired shape or film.
process_recipe.conditions: Mold temperature 20 °C; ensure uniform thickness (≤2 mm for films).

process_recipe.description: Cure the silicone in an oven at 80–100 °C for 2 h to complete cross‑linking.
process_recipe.conditions: Ramp up 5 °C/min; hold temperature for 120 min.

process_recipe.description: Cool the cured part to room temperature, demold, and perform a final inspection for transparency and dimensional accuracy.
